title: Today's Tech Brief: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?
summary: A relaxed walk through the tech headlines, what happened, and the quick read at the end.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?
imageCaption: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI? (theverge.com)

body.text: Today I am keeping this one simple: a quick, easy pass through the tech headlines, with each story getting its own moment. No grand lecture, no giant theory board with red string everywhere. Just what happened, why it is interesting, and a short read at the end.

First up: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?. theverge.com has it. The main detail from the story is this: AI News Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI? Analyses have determined that parts of the Magnifica Humanitas appear to have been written by AI. Analyses have determined that parts of the Magnifica Humanitas appear to have been written by AI. by Jay Peters and Hayden Field May 27, 2026, 12:38 AM UTC Getty Images It’s possible that AI was used to write parts of Pope Leo XIV’s latest encyclical about AI... In plain English, this is the kind of story that tells us what people in this corner of tech are paying attention to right now. The useful thing here is the shape of the tech story underneath it: platforms, infrastructure, security, distribution, and the little power shifts that make the industry feel different a month later.

Next: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation. Here is the headline. The main detail from the story is this: Save this story Save this story When Pope Leo XIV presented his first encyclical on artificial intelligence at the Vatican on Monday, he invited Christopher Olah, cofounder of Anthropic, to speak. The move signaled an unprecedented alliance between the Catholic church and Silicon Valley. But to understand how this partnership came about, we need to go back to Anthropic's founding. Why Anthropic? Anthropic launched i... In plain English, this is the kind of story that tells us what people in this corner of tech are paying attention to right now. The useful thing here is the shape of the tech story underneath it: platforms, infrastructure, security, distribution, and the little power shifts that make the industry feel different a month later.

Also on the board: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI. Here is the headline. The main detail from the story is this: Save this story Save this story An algorithm decides what we see, another filters what we read, and still others enter into the processes that govern work, information, and collective choices. In the encyclical Magnifica Humanitas . the first signed by Pope Leo XIV and published on May 25, artificial intelligence is not viewed as just another technology; it is part of the invisible infrastructure of our contemporary... In plain English, this is the kind of story that tells us what people in this corner of tech are paying attention to right now. The useful thing here is the shape of the tech story underneath it: platforms, infrastructure, security, distribution, and the little power shifts that make the industry feel different a month later.

My read is that today's tech news is less about one giant turning point and more about the texture of the moment. A few platform questions here, a few security or infrastructure questions there, and suddenly the industry has a new little weather pattern to talk through.
